title: The Macro Minute | January 31, 2022

happy monday out there folks it's your
skipper here darius dell to present our
macro minute for monday january 31st
2022 starting with markets we have
stocks mixed this morning commodities up
crypto down dollar flat global bond
yields up big movers we have the russell
2000 off one percent bitcoin off two
percent net gas up six percent on the
economic front friday's december
personal consumption and expenditures
data of the us economy confirmed that
the us economy is in fact losing steam
under the hood
irrespective of omicron so from a
headline basis we saw a headline pce
consumer spending ticked down to 12
percent or sorry contracted at 12
annualized pace uh that's the biggest
contraction we've seen since uh february
2021 services uh slowed to a one percent
annualized pace of growth uh that's the
the slowest rate we've seen since
february of 21 as well and then taking
it to the chart the goods economy the
goods economy hangover is real tickets
borrowing a chart from our around the
horn this weekend we saw goods
consumption real goods real consumer
spinach expenditures on goods contract
at a 32 percent annualized pace in
december christmas month and so clearly
there's something going on here as it
relates to the goods economy hangover
and the over consumption of goods that
may in fact continue to weigh on growth
as we progress throughout 2022 um and
then lastly we had real disposable
personal income as well as real
disposable person real disposable
personal income per capita uh contract
at a three percent annualized pace
that's the slowest number or the biggest
contraction we've seen since the june of
last year
shifting gears a bit uh china's january
pmi data were confirming of our grid
model's view of a mainland economy
slowing to its nadir here in q1 so the
manufacturing the headline official
manufacturing ticked down at 50.1 that's
the lowest print we've seen since
october the non-manufacturing pmi
official take down a 51.1 that's the
lowest print we've seen since august and
then the kashin manufacturing index uh
pmi ticked down to 49.1 that's the uh
the biggest contraction or the lowest
number we've seen since february of
2020. now mind you that was the nadir of
the chinese covet slowdown at that
particular juncture as well so obviously
if you have forward-looking views and we
certainly have some of the best models
in the world in terms of projecting the
rate of change of chinese economy the
global economy the us economy you pick
your economy our models are continuing
to say that that bottom is here in q1 um
from an overbought perspective us
dollars overbought versus the swiss
franc uh in the dollar as overbought
versus the mexican peso this morning uh
dollars or so british pound is oversold
versus the dollar uh looking at the s p
500 that's still bearish fans minus five
point seven percent of downside versus
four point seven percent of upside wti
still neutral vans minus eight percent
of downside versus four percent of
upside bloomberg dollar index broke out
to bullish fans broke back out to
bullish bands this morning uh with 1.7
downside and its probable range versus
only 0.1 percent upside so don't chase
that this morning
10-year treasury yield uh neutral our
bullish fam still minus 6 basis points
of downside versus 21 basis points of
upside bitcoin and ethereum still
bearish fans bitcoin minus 15 percent of
downside versus nine percent of upside
ethereum bearish fam still minus 22
percent of downside versus 14 percent of
upside and then gold uh neutral bands
minus 0.4
of downside versus three point eight
percent of upside so with that dairy
style presenting our macro minute for
